matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Mother > Day 1 > Dinner > Teriyaki Chicken Dinner — Maple-Kissed Bake

node.name: Teriyaki Chicken Dinner — Maple-Kissed Bake
node.short_description: Baked chicken with mixed vegetables and rice glazed in teriyaki sauce for a balanced, savory dinner.
node.meal_suggestion: Meal prep idea: bake a larger batch and portion into 4 servings. For more fiber, swap white rice for brown rice or quinoa. Opt for low-sodium teriyaki sauce or use less sauce. Add sesame seeds and green onions for flavor, and pair with a small side of fruit or leafy greens to enhance balance.
node.nutrition_note: Protein from chicken supports satiety and muscle maintenance; mixed vegetables add fiber, vitamins, and minerals; rice provides energy from carbohydrates. Teriyaki sauce adds flavor but can raise sodium and added sugar; consider using low-sodium teriyaki or moderating sauce amount. For more fiber, substitute brown rice and/or increase vegetable portions.

context_trace.predicate: atom(content =~ "plant-based light grain bowl with grains and mixed vegetables")
context_trace.description: Matched atom(content =~ "plant-based light grain bowl with grains and mixed vegetables") on Teriyaki Chicken Dinner — Maple-Kissed Bake (Score: 0.98)

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Mother > Day 4 > Dinner > Stuffed Bell Peppers — Citrus Fresh Combo

node.name: Stuffed Bell Peppers — Citrus Fresh Combo
node.short_description: Bell peppers stuffed with lean turkey, rice, tomato sauce, and cheddar—a balanced mix of protein, starch, and vegetables.
node.meal_suggestion: Serve one stuffed pepper per person with a simple side salad. For a lighter version, use brown rice and reduced-fat cheddar; choose low-sodium tomato sauce. Bake until peppers are tender.
node.nutrition_note: High in protein from lean ground turkey; includes complex carbs from rice and fiber from peppers. Cheddar adds fat and calcium. Tomato sauce provides lycopene; to manage sodium, use low-sodium sauce and consider brown rice for extra fiber.

context_trace.node_name: Stuffed Bell Peppers — Citrus Fresh Combo
context_trace.predicate: atom(content =~ "plant-based light grain bowl with grains and mixed vegetables")
context_trace.description: Matched atom(content =~ "plant-based light grain bowl with grains and mixed vegetables") on Stuffed Bell Peppers — Citrus Fresh Combo (Score: 0.94)

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Mother > Day 7 > Dinner > Vegetable Soup & Bread — Citrus Fresh Twist

node.name: Vegetable Soup & Bread — Citrus Fresh Twist
node.short_description: A light, satisfying trio of vegetable soup, whole-grain bread, and a side salad with a citrus-inspired twist.
node.meal_suggestion: Balanced light lunch option. For extra protein, add chickpeas, lentils, or a dollop of yogurt. To enhance the Citrus Fresh Twist, consider a citrus vinaigrette on the salad.
node.nutrition_note: Fiber-rich from the vegetables and whole-grain bread; provides complex carbs, and vitamins/minerals with low saturated fat. Protein is modest; sodium depends on the soup—opt for a low-sodium option to support heart health. Side salad adds hydration and micronutrients.

context_trace.node_name: Vegetable Soup & Bread — Citrus Fresh Twist

context_trace.description: Matched atom(content =~ "plant-based light grain bowl with grains and mixed vegetables") on Vegetable Soup & Bread — Citrus Fresh Twist (Score: 0.92)

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Mother > Day 4 > Dinner > Vegetable Soup & Bread — Garden Veg Variation

node.name: Vegetable Soup & Bread — Garden Veg Variation
node.short_description: A plant-forward combination of vegetable soup, whole grain bread, and a side salad for fiber, nutrients, and steady energy.
node.meal_suggestion: Serve 1 cup of vegetable soup with 1 to 2 slices of whole grain bread and a side salad. For extra protein, add beans, lentils, or tofu to the soup or include a protein-rich topping on the salad.
node.nutrition_note: Plant-forward, fiber-rich meal with vegetables, whole grains, and greens. High in dietary fiber and vitamins; provides moderate protein from soup and bread. To keep sodium in check, choose a low-sodium vegetable soup. Optional protein boost (beans, lentils, or tofu) can be added for more protein.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Mother > Day 6 > Dinner > Turkey Meatballs & Pasta — Mild-Spice Set

node.name: Turkey Meatballs & Pasta — Mild-Spice Set
node.short_description: Lean turkey meatballs with whole-wheat pasta in tomato sauce, finished with parmesan for calcium and a mild spice profile.
node.meal_suggestion: Serve with steamed greens (e.g., broccoli or spinach) to add color and fiber. A typical portion: pasta with turkey meatballs in marinara, topped with parmesan.
node.nutrition_note: Lean turkey provides protein; whole-wheat pasta offers fiber and complex carbs; parmesan adds calcium. Tomato marinara adds micronutrients but can be high in sodium—use low-sodium sauce and moderate cheese for a lighter option; pair with vegetables for balance.
